pages.name: Home
pages.description: Landing page introducing the counseling practice and directing users to core actions like booking, finding therapists, groups, assessments, resources, and crisis help. Includes a prominent crisis callout and a footer newsletter signup section.
primary_functions: Provide clear entry points to book an appointment, find a therapist, view group programs, take self-assessments, and access crisis support
Highlight key services (individual counseling, telehealth, groups) and reassure users with brief trust-building content
Display a persistent call-to-action for urgent help that links to crisis_support.html
Offer a footer newsletter signup section (including parenting & families topic and monthly frequency options)

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the primary "Book an Appointment" call-to-action in the hero section
Click the "Find a Therapist" call-to-action card
Click the "Group Programs" call-to-action card
Click the "Self-Assessments" call-to-action card
Click the urgent "Get Help Now"/crisis callout button
Click a "Fees & Insurance" link within homepage content

access_methods.description: Accessed via the main "Home" navigation link
Accessed by visiting the site root or index.html directly

pages.name: Therapists Directory
pages.description: Filterable directory of all therapists for browsing, filtering, and selecting providers for individual work or telehealth consults, including comparison tools.
primary_functions: Filter therapists by specialty/concerns (e.g., Anxiety, Trauma/PTSD), format (In-person, Online/Telehealth), ZIP code, pricing (including sliding scale), rating, and availability (including weekends)
Sort therapist results by criteria such as Next available and Price (Low to High)
Add or remove therapists from a comparison list and open the compare view when exactly two are selected
Open an individual therapist profile for more details and to initiate free 15-minute telehealth consultations

incoming_params.description: Optional specialty filter label such as "Anxiety" or "Trauma/PTSD" to pre-filter the directory
Optional session format filter, e.g., "in_person" or "online"
Optional ZIP code filter for in-person therapists, e.g., "94103"
If set to "1" or "true", limits therapists to those offering sliding-scale pricing
Optional maximum standard session fee for filtering, e.g., 80
Optional minimum rating filter, e.g., 4.5
Optional comma-separated list of days such as "saturday,sunday"
Optional sort mode: "next_available", "price_low_to_high", or "rating_high_to_low"

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a therapist card or "View Profile" button
Click the "Compare (2)" bar/button once exactly two therapists are selected

access_methods.description: Accessed via the "Find a Therapist" navigation link
Accessed with optional query parameters to pre-populate filters

pages.name: Therapist Profile
pages.description: Detailed profile page for a single therapist, including qualifications, specialties, fees, and scheduling options for individual sessions and free consults.
primary_functions: Present therapist details such as bio, specialties (e.g., anxiety, trauma), session formats, fees, sliding scale availability, rating, and schedule overview
Allow users to choose appointment types, including a free 15-minute consultation when available
Open a scheduling tool for the selected appointment type and navigate to the nearest upcoming weekend for telehealth consults
Let users select an available weekend time slot and proceed to the next booking step (appointment_confirm.html)

incoming_params.description: ID of the therapist whose profile should be displayed

params.appointmentId: id returned from createDraftAppointmentFromSlot for the selected slot

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the "Continue"/"Next" button after selecting a free 15-minute consult or other appointment slot

params.appointmentTypeCode: code of a selected appointment type such as "individual_counseling"

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a generic "See more times" or "Book another appointment" link from the profile

access_methods.description: Accessed when a therapistId is provided in the query string
Reached by clicking a therapist card in the directory or comparison view

pages.name: Book Appointment
pages.description: Guided booking page to select appointment type, filter therapists, and choose an available time slot for individual or other counseling sessions.
primary_functions: Let users select an appointment type (e.g., Individual Counseling) as the first step in the booking flow
Offer filters for concerns/specialties (e.g., Anxiety), format (In-person, Online), location (ZIP code input), date range (including next 7 days), and time of day (e.g., Evening after 5:00 pm)
Display a therapist list that can be sorted by Next available and shows a 'View availability' option for each therapist
Show an availability grid for a selected therapist, allowing the user to pick the earliest available time slot at or after a chosen time and proceed to the booking form (appointment_confirm.html)

incoming_params.description: Optional pre-selected appointment type code such as "individual_counseling"
Optional concern or specialty label like "Anxiety" to initialize filters
Optional session format such as "in_person" or "online"
Optional ZIP code to constrain in-person appointment searches, e.g., "94103"
Optional ISO 8601 date indicating the earliest date to search for appointments
Optional ISO 8601 date indicating the latest date to search for appointments
Optional time-of-day filter such as "evening" for after 17:00
Optional flag ("true"/"false") to limit results to weekend slots

params.appointmentId: id returned from createDraftAppointmentFromSlot for the chosen slot

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the "Continue" or "Book" button after selecting an appointment time in the availability grid
Click a "View therapists" link to see matching therapists in the full directory

access_methods.description: Accessed via the "Book an Appointment" navigation link
Accessed with optional query parameters to pre-select appointment type and constraints

pages.name: Appointment Confirmation
pages.description: Final booking form to confirm appointment details and provide contact information before submission.
primary_functions: Display the selected therapist, appointment type, date, and time so the user can verify their choice
Collect client contact details (first name, last name, phone, email) required to finalize the appointment
Allow users to confirm or book the appointment via a 'Confirm Appointment' or 'Book Now' button
Provide a confirmation summary or success message after the appointment is submitted

incoming_params.description: ID of the draft Appointment to display and finalize

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a "Back to home" or similar link after successful confirmation

access_methods.description: Reached after selecting an appointment slot and creating a draft appointment
Requires an appointmentId parameter to load the draft appointment details

pages.name: Therapist Comparison
pages.description: Side-by-side comparison view of selected therapists to help users choose between options based on specialty, pricing, format, and availability.
primary_functions: Display up to two selected therapists side by side with key attributes (specialties, fees, sliding scale, session formats, rating, availability, location)
Highlight pricing details including sliding scale eligibility and typical session fees (e.g., under $80)
Provide quick actions to select one therapist to begin booking or view their full profile
Allow users to remove therapists from the comparison list and return to the directory (therapists.html)

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a "Back to search" or "Return to directory" link

params.therapistId: id of the therapist chosen from the comparison view

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a "View profile" or "Book with this therapist" button under one of the compared therapists

params.appointmentTypeCode: defaulted appointment type code such as "individual_counseling"

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a generic "Start booking" button that leads into the guided booking flow

access_methods.description: Accessed via the "Compare (2)" bar/button on the therapists directory page

pages.name: Pricing & Insurance
pages.description: Overview of session fees, sliding scale options, and accepted insurance plans with sortable details to support cost and coverage decisions.
primary_functions: Explain standard fees for different services and describe sliding scale options and eligibility
Present a table or list of accepted insurance plans with fields such as plan name, copay amount, and notes
Allow users to sort the insurance list by copay from low to high to find the cheapest qualifying plan
Provide a direct path to contact.html for insurance or billing questions

incoming_params.description: Optional sort field for insurance plans, e.g., "copay_asc" to sort by lowest copay first

params.insurancePlanId: id of the plan the user is asking about
params.copayAmount: copay amount of the selected plan

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click an "Ask about this plan" or "Request coverage details" link in the insurance plans table

access_methods.description: Accessed from the "Fees & Insurance" header nav link
Accessed from the "Fees & Insurance" footer link
Can be loaded with an optional sortBy parameter to pre-sort insurance plans

pages.name: Contact
pages.description: Contact form for inquiries about services, scheduling, insurance, crisis support, and other questions.
primary_functions: Offer a reason-for-contact dropdown, including an 'Insurance or Billing' option
Collect user information such as name, email, and phone number
Provide a message text area where users can ask detailed questions, including specifics about an insurance plan and its copay
Submit the inquiry via a 'Submit' or 'Send Message' button and display a confirmation that the message was received

incoming_params.description: Optional contact reason code such as "insurance_billing" used to pre-select the reason dropdown
Optional InsurancePlan.id being referenced in the question
Optional copay amount for the selected plan, pre-filled into the message body

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a "Return to Home" link after successfully sending a message

access_methods.description: Accessed via the "Contact Us" footer navigation link
Accessed via contextual "Contact" or "Get in Touch" links within other pages
Optional parameters pre-populate the reason and insurance plan context

pages.name: Group Programs
pages.description: Listing and filter page for all group therapy programs, including topic and schedule filters such as social anxiety groups starting next month.
primary_functions: List available group programs with key information such as topic (e.g., Social Anxiety), start date, schedule, and format
Allow filtering by group topic/category, including a Social Anxiety option or closest equivalent
Filter groups by start date, including the ability to select the next calendar month
Link to a group’s details page (group_detail.html) for more information and registration options

incoming_params.description: Optional group topic code such as "social_anxiety" or "parenting" to pre-filter results
Optional month identifier like "2026-06" used to filter groups starting in that calendar month
Optional format filter ("in_person", "online", or "hybrid")

params.groupProgramId: id of the group program selected from the list

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a group card or its "View details" link

access_methods.description: Accessed via the "Group Programs" header nav link
Accessed via the "Group Programs" footer link
Can be loaded with topic and startMonth query parameters to pre-filter listings

pages.name: Group Details
pages.description: Detailed page for a specific group program with schedule, description, and registration options for particular time slots.
primary_functions: Show comprehensive information about the selected group, including goals, topics, facilitator(s), fees, and requirements
Display the group’s schedule with specific time slots, including options that run between 6:00 pm and 8:00 pm on weekday evenings
Allow users to select a specific time slot (e.g., 6:00–8:00 pm) via radio button or dropdown
Provide a 'Register' or 'Reserve a Spot' button that leads to the group registration form (group_registration.html)

incoming_params.description: ID of the GroupProgram whose details should be displayed

params.groupSessionId: id of the specific GroupSession (e.g., 18:00–20:00 weekday session) selected for registration

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the "Register" or "Reserve a Spot" button for a chosen time slot

access_methods.description: Accessed with a groupProgramId specifying which group's details to show
Reached by clicking a group from the Group Programs listing

pages.name: Group Registration
pages.description: Registration form for a selected group program and time slot, including participant details and payment option such as pay later.
primary_functions: Confirm the selected group program and chosen schedule (including the 6:00–8:00 pm time slot)
Collect participant information such as first name, last name, and email address
Allow users to specify the number of participants or seats required (e.g., 1)
Offer payment options including a deferred choice (e.g., 'Pay later' or 'Pay at first session') and complete registration via a 'Complete Registration' or 'Submit' button

incoming_params.description: ID of the GroupSession that the user is registering for

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a "Back to Home" or similar link after completing group registration

access_methods.description: Reached from the Group Details page after choosing a specific session time
Requires a groupSessionId in the query string to load the correct registration context

pages.name: Self-Assessments
pages.description: Hub page listing available mental health self-assessment tools with descriptions and disclaimers.
primary_functions: List available self-assessment tools such as Depression (PHQ-9) and other screenings
Provide brief descriptions and estimated time for each assessment to help users choose
Allow users to start a selected assessment (e.g., Depression PHQ-9) by navigating to assessment_detail.html
Offer guidance and disclaimers about using assessments as informational tools rather than diagnoses

params.assessmentCode: code of the selected assessment, e.g., "phq_9"

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the "Start" button for an assessment like Depression (PHQ-9)

access_methods.description: Accessed via the "Self-Assessments" header nav link
Accessed via the "Self-Assessments" footer link

pages.name: Assessment Detail
pages.description: Interactive questionnaire page for a selected self-assessment (e.g., Depression PHQ-9), allowing users to answer and submit for results.
primary_functions: Display all questions for the chosen assessment with clearly labeled answer options (e.g., Not at all, Several days, etc.)
Allow users to answer each item, including using the middle option consistently if they choose
Validate that all questions are answered before submission
Submit responses and navigate to the results page (assessment_results.html) via a 'Submit' or 'Get Results' button

incoming_params.description: Assessment code such as "phq_9" indicating which questionnaire to load

params.assessmentResultId: id of the AssessmentResult returned from submitAssessmentSession

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the "Submit"/"Get Results" button after answering all questions

access_methods.description: Requires assessmentCode to know which assessment to start
Submitting responses transitions to the results page with an assessmentResultId

pages.name: Assessment Results
pages.description: Results page showing assessment outcomes, severity interpretation, recommendations, and a preview of recommended resources.
primary_functions: Present the assessment score with a simple explanation of what the range may indicate
Offer supportive next-step suggestions such as considering counseling, crisis resources, or self-help strategies
Display a 'Recommended Resources' or 'Suggested Articles' section relevant to the assessment (e.g., depression-specific resources)
Provide a 'View all' link or button that leads to a fuller list of recommended articles on resources.html

incoming_params.description: ID of the AssessmentResult whose details and recommendations should be displayed

params.assessmentResultId: current assessmentResultId used to load the full recommended articles list

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the "View all" link/button within the Recommended Resources section
Click an inline crisis-support link if severe results suggest immediate help

access_methods.description: Accessed with an assessmentResultId query parameter to load specific results

pages.name: Resources & Articles
pages.description: List of educational resources and articles, including those recommended after assessments, with save-to-reading-list functionality.
primary_functions: Display a list of recommended articles (e.g., for depression) that can be accessed from the assessment results page
Allow users to save or unsave articles via a 'Save', 'Add to Reading List', or bookmark control on each item
Support filtering or categorization by topic (e.g., depression, anxiety, parenting & families) if desired
Provide links from each article entry to full article content (if implemented) without being required for the core tasks

incoming_params.description: Optional AssessmentResult ID used to load the full set of recommended articles from that result
Optional topic filter such as "depression", "anxiety", or "parenting_families"

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a "Reading List"/"Saved" link or icon to view all saved resources

access_methods.description: Accessed via links from assessment results or other educational sections
Optional query parameters specify topic or associated assessment result

pages.name: Reading List
pages.description: Personal list of saved articles and resources for later reading, stored privately on the device.
primary_functions: Display all articles and resources the user has saved from resources.html or other parts of the site
Allow users to open each saved article or resource in a new view or tab
Enable users to remove items from their reading list when they are no longer needed
Reassure users that their reading list is kept private on their device (implementation detail) and is meant for personal reference

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a "Find more articles" or topic link from within the reading list

access_methods.description: Accessed via the "Reading List" header nav link

pages.name: Crisis Support
pages.description: Immediate help page listing crisis hotlines, steps to take during a crisis, and access to a safety plan tool.
primary_functions: Prominently display instructions for getting immediate help, including calling emergency services when necessary
List local crisis hotline numbers (e.g., county crisis line, youth crisis line) as well as national resources
Encourage users to note down or use two different local hotline numbers for their safety planning
Provide a clear 'Create Safety Plan' or 'My Safety Plan' button linking to safety_plan.html

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the "Create Safety Plan" or "My Safety Plan" button

access_methods.description: Accessed via the "Crisis Support" header nav link
Accessed via the "Crisis Support" footer link

pages.name: Safety Plan
pages.description: Form-based tool for users to create and save a personal safety plan including contacts, warning signs, and coping strategies.
primary_functions: Allow users to record multiple crisis contacts, including local hotline numbers with labels (e.g., 'County Crisis Line', 'Youth Crisis Line')
Provide fields for 'Warning Signs' or 'What I Notice' so users can note early signs of distress (e.g., isolating, not answering messages)
Provide fields for 'Coping Strategies' or 'Things I Can Do' so users can list helpful actions (e.g., going for a walk, calling a friend)
Allow users to save their safety plan via a 'Save Safety Plan' button and optionally view or print the saved plan for quick access

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a "Back to Crisis Support" link after viewing or saving the safety plan

access_methods.description: Accessed via the "Create Safety Plan" button from the Crisis Support page

header_links.description: Start booking an individual or other counseling session
View and register for group therapy programs
View session fees, sliding scale options, and insurance plans
Get immediate help and crisis hotline numbers
Access your saved articles and resources

footer_links.description: Contact form for questions about services, scheduling, and insurance
Information about pricing, sliding scale, and insurance coverage
Overview of current group therapy offerings
Screening tools for common mental health concerns
Local and national crisis hotlines and safety planning
Sign up for email updates and mental health tips, including parenting-focused content
